title: Trial begins for former Central African Republic leader Bozizé over war crimes
description: The UN-backed Special Criminal Court in the Central African Republic will on Tuesday, with foreign judges, begin the trial in absentia of former president François Bozizé over crimes against humanity. Bozizé, 79, who seized power in a 2003 coup before being overthrown 10 years later by rebels, has been living in exile in Guinea-Bissau since March 2023.

title: Iran held by New Zealand in World Cup opener overshadowed by politics
description: Iran twice came from behind to salvage a 2–2 draw against New Zealand in a World Cup opener overshadowed by geopolitical tensions, protests and months of conflict with the United States. The result leaves Team Melli still searching for a breakthrough on football's biggest stage.

description: US Vice President JD Vance said ships will pass through the Strait of Hormuz without tolls under a peace deal signed with Iran, while insisting Tehran must meet its commitments before receiving economic benefits. Officials said the agreement could unlock reconstruction funding, but key details remain to be negotiated. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

title: Swedish intelligence warns of increased Middle East war fallout
description:
STOCKHOLM: A top Swedish intelligence official on Thursday warned of increased threats against American and Israeli interests, as well as Iranian dissidents, in Sweden because of the war in the Middle East.

title: Israel decided to kill Khamenei in November, defense minister says
description:
JERUSALEM: Israel took the decision to kill Iran’s Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in November and was planning to carry out the operation around six months later, Defense Minister Israel Katz said on Thursday.
	Khamenei was killed in the first hours of the US-Israeli air campaign that began on Saturday in the first assassination of a country’s top ruler by an airstrike.

title: Macron calls on Israel, Iran and Hezbollah to prevent Lebanon being embroiled in conflict
description:
LONDON: Emmanuel Macron on Thursday called on Israel and Iran not to embroil Lebanon in the conflict sweeping the Middle East.

	The French president made his plea as panic swept through Beirut after Israel ordered residents to evacuate the southern suburbs of the Lebanese capital and three villages in Lebanon’s eastern Bekaa region.

	Macron said he had drawn up a plan to end hostilities between Israel and the Iran-backed Lebanese militia Hezbol

title: Jordan’s king condemns Iranian attacks in region during talks with Syrian president
description:
LONDON: King Abdullah of Jordan and President Ahmad Al-Sharaa of Syria discussed on Thursday the dangerous developments in the region this week following the US and Israeli attacks against Iran that began on Saturday.

	During their telephone conversation, both leaders emphasized the need for dialogue to prevent further escalation of the regional crisis.

title: Kane to miss Bayern game against Gladbach with calf knock
description:
BERLIN: Harry Kane will miss Bayern Munich’s Bundesliga match against Borussia Moenchengladbach with a calf injury, coach Vincent Kompany said on Thursday.
	The England striker, who has been an ever-present for Bayern this season, should be fit in time for the Champions League last-16 first leg at Atalanta next Tuesday.

title: PSG prepare for Chelsea clash with Monaco rematch
description:
PARIS: Paris Saint-Germain warm up for their Champions League last-16 showdown with Chelsea by once again taking on Monaco on Friday, as coach Luis Enrique looked forward to “the best part of the season” starting.
	PSG have not managed to rediscover the exhilarating form that led them to Champions League glory for the first time in their history last season, but they come into March still on course to retain their French and European titles.

title: Malaysia appoints new anti-graft chief
description:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia appointed on Saturday a former high court judge as the country’s new anti-graft czar to replace its outgoing chief who was dogged by allegations of abuse of power and illegal shareholding.
	The appointment of Abdul Halim Aman comes as Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim’s government faces increased scrutiny on promises made to tackle corruption in the Southeast Asian nation.

title: Chinese panda pair headed to US zoo
description:
BEIJING: Two giant pandas from China are headed to Atlanta on a new 10-year conservation deal.
	Pandas Ping Ping, a male, and Fu Shuang, a female, will live at Zoo Atlanta, China Wildlife Conservation Association said in a statement Friday.
	The organization signed the research agreement with the United States zoo last year, it said, noting the deal continues “the ‘panda bond’ shared by the people of both nations for more than 20 years.”

title: Despite Iran tensions, King Charles III will follow his mother’s lead in celebrating US-UK bonds
description:
LONDON: The challenge for King Charles III as he embarks on this week’s state visit to the US is, as always, to live up to his mother’s example.
	The late Queen Elizabeth II wowed Congress in 1991 with a speech that celebrated the shared democratic traditions of Britain and the United States, quoted Abraham Lincoln, Franklin D. Roosevelt and Ralph Waldo Emerson, and highlighted the deep bonds between the two nations.

title: Australia refuses repatriation help for citizens in Syria camp
description:
SYDNEY: Australia refused to help repatriate citizens related to suspected Daesh militants on Saturday, following a report that some Australian families are trying to return home from a camp in Syria.
	National broadcaster ABC said four Australian women and nine of their children and grandchildren had left the Roj camp in northeastern Syria on Friday, citing the camp director.
	Syrian forces were taking them to Damascus to help them fly back to A

title: Saudi Arabia’s events sector push on track despite disruptions, says industry veteran
description:
RIYADH: Saudi Arabia’s push to position itself as a global events hub remains firmly on track despite geopolitical disruptions, according to one of the leading figures in the industry.

	The US-Israel war with Iran has led to disruptions to many of the Kingdom’s flagship forums, with the Saudi Arabian Grand Prix 2026, the LEAP technology conference, and Automechanika Riyadh among those to be postponed.

title: Startup Wrap: MENA startups draw capital as AI bets continue to accelerate
description:
RIYADH: Startups across the Middle East and North Africa saw new funding rounds over the past week, with acquisitions, and partnerships across artificial intelligence, fintech, gaming, and digital infrastructure.

	Saudi Arabia-based Signit raised $15 million in a series A round led by Raed Ventures, with participation from STV, Seedra Ventures, Takamol Ventures, and Suhail Ventures.

title: US military says it struck vessel in Eastern Pacific, killing two
description:
WASHINGTON: The US military said on Friday it struck a vessel in the Eastern Pacific, killing two people, in the latest such attack, condemned by rights groups as “extrajudicial killings” and described by Washington as targeting “narco-terrorists.”
	The US Southern Command alleged that the vessel struck on Friday was operated by “Designated Terrorist Organizations” that ‌it did ‌not identify.

description:

title: Mixed reactions in Lebanon and Israel over Iran-US ceasefire
description: The United States and Iran reached an initial agreement Monday that would extend their shaky ceasefire and lead to the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, but significant challenges remain to ending the war, including whether Israel will continue its offensive in Lebanon. The deal was met by sceptism by some in Beirut who doubt if it will really bring an end to the destruction, while in Israel some would like to see the war continue.

title: G7 summit: 'Pressure will be on leaders to show support for Ukraine'

title: Israel 'has had its hands forced' by US-Iran deal
description: The US and Iran said they have agreed terms to end their war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Though the text has not yet been published, its apparent call for hostilities to cease more broadly could be tricky for Israel, which was not part of the negotiations and has been waging war in Lebanon against the Iran-aligned Hezbollah group. FRANCE 24’s Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.

They also reafirmed that preventing Iran from acquiring nuclear weapons must remain a priority.

title: US-Iran deal 'what Tehran wanted'
description: US and Iran agreed to an “immediate and permanent” end to military operations under a surprise memorandum of understanding announced via mediator Pakistan on Sunday. These were both key points demanded by Tehran before agreeing to any deal, reports France 24's correspondent Reza Sayah.

title: The role of journalism in a post-truth age – with Abigail Pogrebin - Sponsored Content
description:
With the marketplace of ideas outpacing our ability to discern truth, who still cares about keeping up with journalistic standards?
The post The role of journalism in a post-truth age  with Abigail Pogrebin appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: 14 said arrested as hundreds of protesters clash at London Israeli real estate event
description:
Event held in synagogue drew opposition from lawmakers, activists who claimed event marketed settlement homes, which organizers deny; Board of Deputies: 'We are deeply disturbed'
The post 14 said arrested as hundreds of protesters clash at London Israeli real estate event appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Haredi protesters rally outside chief justice’s home to oppose draft dodger’s arrest
description:
Protest is latest to target a High Court official, after home of Amit's deputy Noam Sohlberg hit by riot earlier this month; mass Haredi demonstrations have also blocked roads
The post Haredi protesters rally outside chief justice’s home to oppose draft dodgers arrest appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Defense minister: Israel staying in south Lebanon; if Iran strikes, we’ll hit it ‘with full force’
description:
Lebanese media reports IDF drone strike, artillery fire in south * Trump calls Netanyahu ‘very difficult guy,’ says attacks on Iran could resume if nuclear deal not reached
The post Defense minister: Israel staying in south Lebanon; if Iran strikes, well hit it with full force appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Torn Iranian Americans plan protests, watch parties for team’s World Cup opener
description:
Many are upset over FIFA rule barring pre-revolution lion-and-sun flag from stadiums; one fan says tournament doesn't represent the people of Iran: 'This is Team Islamic Republic'
The post Torn Iranian Americans plan protests, watch parties for teams World Cup opener appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: The Knicks won on 6/13. Some Jewish fans think that’s more than a coincidence.
description:
New Yorkers, pointing out that the Torah has 613 commandments, celebrate 'miracle' of long-time-coming win for US's most Jewish city 
The post The Knicks won on 6/13. Some Jewish fans think that’s more than a coincidence. appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: PA’s Abbas says oft-canceled presidential elections to be held in 2027
description:
Palestinian Authority's leader has now remained in power for 20 years, 15 years beyond the original date on which he was due to seek re-election
The post PAs Abbas says oft-canceled presidential elections to be held in 2027 appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: US plans major cut to jets, warships for NATO operations in Europe, NYT reports
description: The United States reportedly plans to significantly reduce the aircraft and warships it makes available for NATO operations in Europe.

title: France prepares Ukraine-inspired ‘kill web’ for battlefield awareness
description: The kill web will be part of the broader Arcadia AI-powered battlefield command and control structure, a top French commander said.

title: Putin’s paranoia: AI espionage pushes Kremlin to reinforce security measures
description: Russia’s security services temporarily disconnected a special surveillance system protecting Putin and his close circle in the wake of the US-Israeli killing of Iran’s supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the Financial Times reported. The alleged incident highlights the heightened security threat of AI video analysis for the Russian leadership, said Ksenia Ermoshina, an expert on surveillance and censorship technologies.

title: World Cup 2026: Sweden make statement with commanding victory over Tunisia
description: Sweden claimed a commanding 5-1 victory over Tunisia in Monterrey, Mexico, taking a significant step toward Group F progression. Gyökeres, Isak, Ayari and Svanberg found the net for Graham Potter's side, with Omar Rekik's consolation goal proving insufficient to salvage a point for the North African underdogs.

title: In Pictures: Trump marks 80th birthday with White House UFC spectacle
description: US President Donald Trump marked his 80th birthday with a highly unusual White House spectacle, hosting a UFC cage fight on the South Lawn just hours after announcing a memorandum of understanding with Iran, in a made-for-television display blending politics, sport and patriotic pageantry.

title: G7 summit opens in France with Ukraine war, new US-Iran agreement on agenda
description: The memorandum of understanding between the United States and Iran was announced on the eve of the G7 summit in Evian-les-Bains, France, where leaders from some of the world’s wealthiest nations will begin meeting Monday. Gathered in the Alps through Wednesday, they will also discuss the war in Ukraine and global economic imbalances.

title: Middle East live: Macron says G7 leaders to discuss 'long-term reopening' of Hormuz strait
description: Ahead of the signing of a US-Iranian memorandum of understanding scheduled for Friday in Geneva, G7 leaders will meet in Evian on Monday to discuss “the implications of this agreement, support for Lebanon, the long-term reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, and, of course, the conclusion of an agreement on Iran’s nuclear and ballistic missile programs,” according to French President Emmanuel Macron. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
